claimText: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has granted Self-Regulatory Organisation (SRO) status to the Finance Industry Development Council (FIDC).
evidenceExcerpt: The RBI has granted Self-Regulatory Organisation (SRO) status to the Finance Industry Development Council (FIDC) to oversee NBFCs.
examUse: Statement-based question to test knowledge of recent regulatory appointments.
distractorHooks: Naming a different regulatory body like SEBI or IRDAI instead of RBI.
Naming a different industry body like FICCI or CII instead of FIDC.

claimText: The Finance Industry Development Council (FIDC) as an SRO is tasked with overseeing Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs).
evidenceExcerpt: The RBI has granted Self-Regulatory Organisation (SRO) status to the Finance Industry Development Council (FIDC) to oversee NBFCs.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching the SRO with the entities it regulates.
distractorHooks: Suggesting FIDC oversees commercial banks or cooperative banks instead of NBFCs.
Misstating the function as funding or licensing instead of overseeing.

claimText: The recognition of SROs for regulated entities is governed by the RBI’s Omnibus Framework of 2024.
evidenceExcerpt: This is governed by the RBI’s 2024 Omnibus Framework, which mandates that an SRO must be a not-for-profit company...
examUse: Statement-based question about the legal basis for SROs.
distractorHooks: Mentioning an incorrect year for the framework (e.g., 2023, 2022).
Attributing the framework to the Ministry of Finance or a parliamentary act instead of the RBI.

claimText: An SRO must be established as a not-for-profit company registered under Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013.
evidenceExcerpt: An SRO shall be setup as a not-for-profit company registered under Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the legal structure requirement for an SRO.
distractorHooks: Stating it can be a for-profit company.
Citing an incorrect section of the Companies Act (e.g., Section 25).
Suggesting registration under a different act, like the Societies Registration Act.
